etymology.description: Phonetic 又(yòu, "again") + semantic inference from ancient form of 有(yǒu, "to have")
etymology.interpretation: The character 㞢 is an ancient variant of 有(yǒu, "to have"). As a phono-semantic compound, it combines the phonetic element 又(yòu, "again") with a semantic connection to the idea of possession or existence. It highlights how components related to form (又) play a role in shaping meaning by suggesting possession.
